Single-tree policy interpreter

Source: R/cate_interpreter.R
SingleTreePolicyInterpreter.Rd

Interprets a treatment assignment policy based on a CATE estimator by fitting a single decision tree that predicts the best treatment (control vs treat) from covariates. Aligns with EconML econml.cate_interpreter.SingleTreePolicyInterpreter.

Usage

SingleTreePolicyInterpreter(
  risk_level = NULL,
  risk_seeking = FALSE,
  max_depth = NULL,
  min_samples_split = 2L,
  min_samples_leaf = 1L,
  min_weight_fraction_leaf = 0,
  max_features = NULL,
  min_balancedness_tol = 0.45,
  min_impurity_decrease = 0,
  random_state = NULL
)

Arguments

risk_level

numeric or NULL. If NULL, point CATE is used; if set (e.g. 0.05), the lower (risk_seeking=FALSE) or upper (risk_seeking=TRUE) end of a confidence interval can be used (requires estimator to support intervals).

risk_seeking

logical (default FALSE). If risk_level is set, use upper (TRUE) or lower (FALSE) end of the interval as effect.

max_depth

integer or NULL. Maximum depth of the policy tree.

min_samples_split

integer (default 2).

min_samples_leaf

integer (default 1).

min_weight_fraction_leaf

numeric (default 0).

max_features

integer, character, or NULL.

min_balancedness_tol

numeric in [0, 0.5] (default 0.45). Minimum fraction of samples that must fall on each side of a split.

min_impurity_decrease

numeric (default 0).

random_state

integer or NULL.

Value

Object of class SingleTreePolicyInterpreter.

See also

RCausalML-package

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Basic usage
# SingleTreePolicyInterpreter(...)
} # }
